page: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 system memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the c6a.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU scores, and software compilation using Clang. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block read operations reaching top-tier status, while uncached large-block read and write speeds rank in the strong tier. Cryptographic operations perform exceptionally well, as evidenced by top-tier results in Passmark CPU encryption tests, supported by hardware-level AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c6a.32xlarge offers high compute density and strong memory bandwidth relative to its hardware class, making it a viable option for compute-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be handled externally. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput Redis database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and a 3.6 GHz clock speed. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, offering a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. In benchmark evaluations, the c6a.32xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and cryptographic execution, alongside strong single-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This hardware profile is highly suited for compute-heavy, multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput Redis databases, and static web serving.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 128 vCPUs and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for intensive multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
Equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz with no local storage
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU, encryption, and software compilation performance
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations
